sonnet_review.biasedText: The year is 2025. You froze your eggs a decade ago, after your live-in boyfriend dumped you on your 30th birthday. You're about to start your fifteenth round of IVF, and if this one doesn't work, you have a color-coded list of surrogacy agencies to call.
sonnet_review.reason: While I didn't flag this specific passage, it is defensible. The opening hypothetical is constructed with deliberately exaggerated and negative details (dumped on 30th birthday, fifteenth round of IVF) to evoke a negative emotional response toward reproductive technology.
I flagged this same text as Spin. The detection of loaded word choice is equivalent to my finding.

sonnet_review.biasedText: The ad's suggestion that lost loved ones can be rendered obsolete in minutes by soulless video game characters is repulsive and deeply disrespectful to the memories of the dead.
sonnet_review.reason: I flagged this same passage as Opinion Statements Presented as Fact. The detection captures the same bias instance, focusing on the subjective adjectives within it.

sonnet_review.biasedText: No one wants a creepy Minecraft Grandma.
sonnet_review.reason: I flagged 'creepy Minecraft Grandma' for the subjective qualifier 'creepy.' Sonnet's detection covers the broader claim as an opinion stated as fact, which is also valid.

sonnet_review.biasedText: We've been lusting after it since Eden, desiring to play at being God. It's the same insidious desire that drives many to assisted reproductive technologies.
sonnet_review.reason: I flagged the 'insidious desire' portion. Sonnet's framing as 'mind reading' is a reasonable alternative classification that captures the same bias of attributing negative motivations to people who use ART.

sonnet_review.biasedText: While IVF, gamete donation, and surrogacy are billed as compassionate treatments to help earnest couples struggling with fertility, they are often used to facilitate the lifestyles of women who could have had children naturally but chose to delay doing so by decades
sonnet_review.reason: I flagged a similar portion as an unsubstantiated claim. Sonnet's 'Slant' classification capturing the structural dismissal of the pro-ART perspective is also valid and complementary.
I didn't flag this specific phrase independently, though I noted 'cheap digital counterfeit' in the broader context of my other detections. It is a defensible detection of loaded adjectives.

sonnet_review.biasedText: The destructive methods of assisted reproductive technologies result in life being valued less, not more.
sonnet_review.reason: I flagged this identical passage with the same bias type.

sonnet_review.biasedText: But they ultimately strip her of both body and soul, reducing her to a Tamagotchi pet.
sonnet_review.reason: I flagged the 'Tamagotchi pet' comparison as Spin. Sonnet's detection captures the same passage with a complementary bias classification.

sonnet_review.biasedText: Twenty-first-century magicians promise to circumvent the physical design of the natural order, from childbearing to the sex binary. Like Worthy's app, their cheap attempts are abusing, not augmenting, the real thing.
sonnet_review.reason: I flagged substantially the same passage as Spin. The detection is equivalent.
